things.name: Bunratty Castle & Folk Park
things.description: Restored 15th-century tower castle with medieval banquets and a 26-acre open-air folk museum depicting rural Irish life

things.name: Dromoland Castle
things.description: Magnificent 16th-century castle of the O'Brien clan, now a five-star hotel set in 450 acres of parkland on Lough Dromoland

things.name: Cliffs of Moher
things.description: Ireland's most visited natural attraction — dramatic sea cliffs rising 214 m above the Atlantic, part of the Burren & Cliffs of Moher UNESCO Geopark

things.name: King John's Castle
things.description: Landmark 13th-century Anglo-Norman fortress on King's Island in Limerick city, with interactive exhibits on medieval life

things.name: The Burren
things.description: Vast limestone karst landscape of unparalleled natural beauty — unique wildflowers, ancient dolmens and Ailwee Cave nestled in the UNESCO Geopark

things.name: Ennis Friary
things.description: Remarkably well-preserved 13th-century Franciscan friary in the heart of Ennis, the county town of Clare, with intricate medieval carvings

things.name: Craggaunowen — The Living Past
things.description: Open-air museum bringing prehistoric Ireland to life with a reconstructed crannog, ring fort and Tim Severin's replica Atlantic currach

things.name: Quin Abbey
things.description: One of Ireland's best-preserved Franciscan friaries (1433), built over a Norman castle with a tranquil cloister and tower to climb

things.name: Loop Head Peninsula
things.description: Wild Atlantic Way headland at Ireland's south-western tip — Loop Head Lighthouse, sea stacks, dolphin watching and spectacular cliff walks

things.name: Killaloe & Brian Boru Heritage Centre
things.description: Charming heritage village on the shores of Lough Derg, birthplace of High King Brian Boru, with a 12th-century cathedral and vibrant waterside village
